components.name: Warehouse_shelf.cs
components.description: Representation of the data of each shelf in the warehouse and of the orders.

components.name: Warehouse_node.cs
components.description: Information of each node, its edges and its direction of circulation allowed in X and Z axis.

components.name: NodesRow.cs
components.description: Edges of nodes information, to automatically generate allowed connections between the warehouse nodes without need to specify them manually.

components.name: Warehouse.cs
components.description: Initialization of the robots and the nodes of the warehouse.

components.name: Warehouse_orders.cs
components.description: Assignment of tasks to each robot in a coordinated way.

components.name: OptimalRoute.cs
components.description: Backtracking algorithm for calculating the optimal path between nodes.

components.name: CameraControl.cs
components.description: Camera movement to allow the simulation viewer to move around the warehouse comfortably.

components.name: Warehouse_training.cs
components.description: Automatic training to modify the weights of the robot assignment formula to the optimal values that reduce total processing time between all robots.

components.name: Robot_wheels.cs
components.description: Control of wheels, servo-motors and its speed.

components.name: Robot.cs
components.description: Robot control with kinematics functions, trajectory following systems, collision avoidance strategy, variables about the state of the robot and picking up and unloading of boxes procedures.

components.name: Warehouse_box.cs
components.description: Control of the gravity of the boxes to be able to pick up them.

components.name: Robot_actuator.cs
components.description: Control of the pneumatic actuator of the robot.

components.name: Robot_arm.cs
components.description: Scalable script to control the joints of the robotic arm and its kinematics.

components.name: Robot_rightSensor.cs
components.description: Proximity sensor simulation capable of detecting objects in front of and to the right of the robot.

comparison.explanation: The GroundTruth diagram focuses on the internal components of a Unity simulation, detailing various scripts that handle warehouse operations, robot control, and optimization algorithms. It is highly detailed with specific components and their roles in the simulation. The GeneratedImage diagram, on the other hand, provides a broader view of a system integrating CoppeliaSim, Unity, web interfaces, and cloud services. It highlights the interaction between different environments and technologies rather than focusing on internal components. There are no common components or connectors between the two diagrams, as they represent different levels and aspects of system architecture. The GroundTruth is more detailed in terms of specific functionalities within a simulation, while the GeneratedImage shows a higher-level integration of different systems. Based on its accuracy and completeness, the GeneratedImage would be rated 6 out of 10. While it is well-organized and covers multiple aspects of a system, it lacks the detailed component-level information present in the GroundTruth diagram.
